title: Argentinian Beef Empanadas
description: A classic Argentinian dish, these beef empanadas are filled with tender beef and spices. Serve as a snack or light meal.

ingredients.name: Ground Beef
ingredients.notes: 80/20 lean to fat ratio works well

instructions: In a large skillet, cook the ground beef over medium-high heat until browned.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add the diced chorizo and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Stir in the Worcestershire sauce (if using).
Let the mixture cool to room temperature.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch.
Use a round cookie cutter or the rim of a glass to cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon of the beef mixture in the center of each dough circle.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Fold the dough in half over the filling, pressing the edges together to seal.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the remaining egg wash.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
